story.text: As bdo, I need something that makes a VS Code workspace and keeps it up to date and live, so I can open my Explorer and see each live work directory as a navigable root without managing anything, because I said 'all I need is something that makes a vscode workspace, and makes sure it's up to date and live.'

briefing.headline: A live VS Code workspace projected from the forest fold
briefing.value: The forest fold (done-line 0186) already folds the whole tree of in-flight work into one decorated model; this projects that model into VS Code's multi-root .code-workspace shape — base roots first (the viewport as 'ontum', then ../gallery/../holonsearch if present), then each LIVE/in-review worktree as a navigable root decorated by status (live / in-review), with its PR number and short branch. A SessionStart hook regenerates the file every session, so the Explorer view stays current with no effort.
briefing.why_now: bdo asked for exactly this and nothing more: a generated workspace that stays live. The forest model is built; the workspace is a thin, deterministic projection of it plus a fail-open regen hook. Without it, the whole-tree forest is a FOREST.md read, not a thing he can actually open and navigate in his editor.
briefing.if_accepted: ontum-forest.code-workspace is the live, auto-regenerated workspace: open it once and every live worktree is a root in the Explorer, refreshed at each session start. Live-session detail inside each root and a served view ride later pieces.
briefing.if_rejected: The forest stays a FOREST.md read; bdo still navigates worktrees by hand, and his single physical checkout stays the load-bearing surface.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: --workspace is a pure-stdlib, read-only projection of the existing forest fold that writes only one gitignored live cache (never committed, never truth). Reverting is deleting one mode and one hook line; no truth moves, no new write path on the log.
briefing.mechanism: loop/forest.py gains a --workspace mode (+ --out, --hook): it senses the forest (the same fold as render), then build_workspace projects the model into {folders, settings} — base roots from the PRIMARY checkout first, then each worktree whose status is live-worktree or in-review (the viewport and stranded/merged worktrees excluded) as a decorated root. The default out-path follows the viewport, so a SessionStart hook (--workspace --hook, fail-open exit 0) regenerates the one file bdo opens. The §10 teeth live in the inclusion filter, proven non-vacuous in tests/test_forest_workspace.py.

source_artifacts: bdo 2026-06-22: 'all I need is something that makes a vscode workspace, and makes sure it's up to date and live.'
